name: EGCG
summary: EGCG is best framed around green-tea catechin context, antioxidant polyphenol activity, cardiometabolic marker research, and fat-oxidation support, with safety screening for liver disease, high-dose extracts, and stimulant-sensitive users.
description: EGCG is best framed around green-tea catechin context, antioxidant polyphenol activity, cardiometabolic marker research, and fat-oxidation support, with safety screening for liver disease, high-dose extracts, and stimulant-sensitive users.
safetyNotes: High-dose isolated EGCG can raise liver-safety concerns; avoid aggressive dosing.
contraindications: High-dose extract use above roughly 800 mg EGCG daily carries a documented risk of drug-induced liver injury or hepatitis
avoid in pre-existing liver disease or with other hepatotoxic medications
take with food rather than on an empty stomach to reduce liver-injury risk
concentrated extracts often retain caffeine content so stimulant-sensitive users or those with anxiety or insomnia should use caution
may interact with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications or certain blood pressure drugs
pregnancy or breastfeeding caution due to insufficient safety data.
